recalls.Description: Aisstxoer adult bicycle helmets have been recalled due to safety concerns that may lead to severe head injuries or fatalities. These helmets, available in size large for head circumferences between 22.8 to 24.4 inches, are primarily pink with black padding, straps, and a buckle. The model number 'GH018L' can be found inside the helmet, while the brand name and size are on the packaging label.
recalls.Title: Aisstxoer Adult Bicycle Helmets Recalled Due to Serious Head Injury Risks; Sold by YXTDZ Store on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers should reach out to YXTDZ Store via email at yxtdzamz@126.com and visit the 'Recalls and Product Safety Alerts' section on their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Aisstxoer Adult Bike Helmet (pink)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025 for approximately $25.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshiyongxintaidianziyouxiangongsi (Shenzhen Yongxintai Electronics Co., Ltd.), dba, Yxtdz Store, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for impact protection, posing serious injury or death risks in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease use of the recalled helmets and contact YXTDZ Store for a full refund. Consumers must destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to yxtdzamz@126.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Aisstxoer adult bicycle helmets recalled due to serious injury risks. Contact YXTDZ Store for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: ProRider has issued a recall for several models of bicycle helmets, including the Economy, Bike Helmets with turn ring, Black Foam BMX Helmet, and Toddler Bike Helmets. These helmets were available in various colors such as blue, green, red, black, and purple. Key details including model numbers, manufacturing dates, and serial numbers can be found on a label inside each helmet.
recalls.Title: ProRider Bicycle Helmets Recalled Due to Serious Head Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact ProRider at 800-642-3123 from 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, email org@prorider.com or visit www.prorider.com and click on the "Recall" section for further information.

Retailers.Name: Distributed by Wisconsin Bike Federation, Kiwanis Club, and Foster Love across the nation, and available for purchase online at ProRider.com from June 2022 to May 2023, priced between $5 and $18.

Hazards.Name: The helmets do not meet mandatory safety standards, failing to provide adequate protection in crashes, which poses a significant risk of head injuries or fatalities.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets immediately and reach out to ProRider for a full refund. To complete the refund process, destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to org@prorider.com.

recalls.metaDescription: ProRider recalls several bicycle helmet models due to safety risks, urging consumers to stop use and seek refunds.

recalls.Description: Todson is recalling Concord-branded 360 Degree Rechargeable Light-Up bike helmets due to safety violations that could lead to serious head injuries. These helmets, designed for large sizes fitting head circumferences of approximately 22.8 to 24 inches, feature a black exterior with an integrated LED lighting system. The brand name 'Concord' is displayed on the back of the helmet, which also includes adjustable black straps and a plastic knob for fit adjustments.
recalls.Title: Todson Recalls Concord 360 Degree Rechargeable Light-Up Bike Helmets Due to Serious Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled helmets and contact Todson at 800-278-2565 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at 360concordhelmet@todson.com. For more details, visit todson.com/360-concord-recall or go to www.Todson.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Concord 360 Degree Rechargeable Light-Up Bike Helmet

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores nationwide and online at Walmart.com from January 2025 through September 2025 for approximately $30.

Hazards.Name: The helmets do not meet mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, which may result in inadequate protection during a crash, leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets immediately and contact Todson for a full refund. Helmets should be destroyed by cutting off the straps. Photos of the cut straps can be sent to 360concordhelmet@todson.com for a refund. Proper disposal of the lithium-ion battery is crucial; do not dispose of it in regular trash or recycling. Check with local hazardous waste facilities for safe disposal options.

recalls.metaDescription: Todson recalls Concord bike helmets due to serious injury risks. Immediate action required for refunds and safe disposal.

recalls.Description: SAMIT is recalling its youth multi-purpose helmets designed for children due to safety concerns. These helmets fit head circumferences of approximately 21 to 23 inches and are available in colors including 'crack' blue, 'crack' red, 'crack' black, and pure black, featuring a cracked paint design. They include gray padding, black straps, and a black and red buckle, along with an adjustable black plastic knob at the back.
recalls.Title: Recall of SAMIT Youth Multi-Purpose Helmets Due to Serious Head Injury Risks; Sold on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Samit Outdoors via email at usjialebei@foxmail.com.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2025 through November 2025, priced between $28 and $33.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshilonghuaqujialebeibaihuoshanghang (Jialebei Department Store, Longhua District, Shenzhen) dba Samit Outdoor, of China

Hazards.Name: These helmets do not meet the mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, failing to ensure proper positional stability and certification. This deficiency may lead to inadequate protection during a crash, resulting in a heightened risk of severe injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled helmets immediately and contact Samit Outdoor for a full refund. To dispose of the helmet safely, consumers should cut off the straps. Refund requests can be submitted by emailing photos of the destroyed helmet to usjialebei@foxmail.com.

recalls.metaDescription: SAMIT recalls youth helmets due to serious injury risks. Stop using and contact for a refund. Learn more about the recall.

recalls.Description:
Semfri is recalling its children's multi-purpose helmets due to safety concerns that could lead to serious head injuries. These helmets, available in medium size (M) with a head circumference of approximately 20.5 to 22.8 inches, were sold in various colors including orange, black, blue, navy blue, pink, red, and white. Each helmet features black padding, straps, and a buckle, with a fitting adjustment knob located at the back. The size is indicated on a black circular label inside the helmet.

The recall is prompted by the helmets' failure to meet mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, particularly regarding impact absorption, stability, labeling, and certification. This non-compliance raises significant safety risks, as the helmets may not adequately protect users during accidents, potentially leading to severe injuries or fatalities.
recalls.Title: Semfri Children's Helmets Recalled for Safety Violations and Risk of Head Injury
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the helmets immediately and reach out to Semfri for a full refund. To dispose of the helmet, cut the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to Semfri_recall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Semfri Multi-Purpose Helmet - front view

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, posing serious injury or death risks.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets and contact Semfri for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Semfri recalls children's helmets due to safety violations, posing serious injury risks. Immediate action required for refunds.

recalls.Description: R.X.Y has issued a recall for their adult bicycle helmets due to non-compliance with safety standards, which may lead to serious head injuries in the event of an accident. These helmets are designed to fit head circumferences between 22.44 and 24.41 inches and feature a blue and black color scheme with gray padding and reflective straps. The item number 'Kx012' can be found in the helmet's instruction manual.
recalls.Title: R.X.Y Adult Bicycle Helmets Recalled for Safety Violations and Head Injury Risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets immediately and contact R.X.Y for a full refund. Consumers should destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to RXY-recall@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: R.X.Y recalls adult bicycle helmets due to safety violations, posing serious injury risks. Contact for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: YooxArmor is recalling its multi-purpose kids' helmets due to significant safety concerns. These helmets, available in size small (S), feature a pink design adorned with unicorns, rainbows, and stars, complemented by black padding and straps. A distinctive black and red knob at the back allows for fitting adjustments, and 'YooxArmor' is printed on the rear of the helmet.
recalls.Title: YooxArmor Kids' Helmets Recalled Due to Serious Head Injury Risks; Non-Compliant with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, reach out to YooxArmor via email at service@yooxarmor.com or through their Amazon Seller Profile: YooxArmor.

Distributors.Name: Yiwu Shi Yuxing Keji Youxiangongsi, dba YooxArmor, of China

Hazards.Name: These helmets do not meet the mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, failing in impact attenuation, positional stability, labeling, and certification. This non-compliance increases the risk of serious injury or death in the event of an accident.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the helmets immediately and contact YooxArmor for a full refund. To facilitate the refund, consumers should destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps and can send photos of the altered helmet to service@yooxarmor.com.

recalls.Description: The recall includes various models of 509 Tactical helmets, specifically the Tactical 3.0 (Youth, Mountain, and Trail) and Tactical 2.0 (Youth and Mountain) helmets, all manufactured in 2023. Additionally, it affects several models equipped with Fidlock® technology, including the Tactical 2.0, Altitude 2.0, Delta R3L, Delta R4, Delta V, Mach III, Mach IV, and Mach V, produced from 2020 onwards. These helmets feature the 509 logo and the name 'FIVE O NINE' or 'Polaris' on the respective models. The helmets may not provide adequate protection in the event of an accident, leading to potential head injuries.
recalls.Title: 509 Recalls Multiple Helmet Models Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact 509 at 877-743-3509 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or email support@ride509.com. More information can be found at https://ride509.com/pages/recalls or by visiting www.ride509.com and selecting 'Safety Recalls' under the 'Support' section. For Polaris Industries, call 800-765-2747 during the same hours or visit www.polaris.com and navigate to 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section. Polaris customers can also reach the owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through an online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Injuries.Name: The firm received one incident where the rider's helmet became detached during a crash, resulting in a concussion.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.ride509.com and www.polaris.com, and through 509 and Polaris dealers nationwide from January 2020 to May 2025, priced between $140 and $900.

Importers.Name: Teton Outfitters LLC, dba 509, a Polaris subsidiary, of Rigby, Idaho

Hazards.Name: Insufficient protection in the event of a crash, leading to head injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled helmets immediately. Contact 509 for refund, credit, or replacement options for Tactical 3.0 and Tactical 2.0 helmets. Polaris customers can also choose similar options for their helmets. No proof of purchase is necessary. Discard recalled helmets in household trash after confirming the recall and following disposal instructions on the website. Polaris will provide inspection instructions for Fidlock® helmets.

recalls.metaDescription: 509 recalls several helmet models due to head injury risks. Contact for refunds, credits, or replacements. Safety first!

recalls.Description:
Bell Sports is recalling several models of children's and adult bicycle helmets due to non-compliance with federal safety standards. The affected models include the Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy for children, as well as the Cadence and Passage for adults. These helmets were available in various colors and designs, making them appealing to a wide range of consumers. The model number and manufacture date can be found on a sticker inside each helmet.

Consumers are advised to stop using these helmets immediately as they may not provide adequate protection during a crash, increasing the risk of head injuries. Bell Sports is facilitating a full refund process for all affected customers, and it is important to follow the instructions provided for safe disposal of the helmets.
recalls.Title: Bell Sports Recalls Children's and Adult Bicycle Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Bell Sports at 800-456-2355 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, or visit https://www.thebellgarage.com/bell-sports-helmet-recall-notice-axle-cadence-rev-passage-and-frenzy-models/ for more details.

Products.Name: Axle Children's Bicycle Helmet
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 31,200 (In addition, about 480 were sold in Canada)

Manufacturers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., of Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Target, Academy Sports+ Outdoors, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, FredMeyer.com, Academy.com, and Target.com from September 2024 through May 2025 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with CPSC safety standards increases the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets and contact Bell Sports for a full refund. Destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps and submit a photo to obtain the refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls various children's and adult bicycle helmets due to head injury risks. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description:
NQDTPBOR is recalling its multi-purpose helmets designed for teenagers due to non-compliance with federal safety regulations. These helmets come in small and medium sizes, accommodating head circumferences of approximately 18 to 20.5 inches and 20.5 to 22 inches, respectively. Available in various colors including blue, pink, purple, green, and black, each helmet features black padding, straps, and a black and red buckle, with the brand name "NQDTPBOR" displayed on the side.

The recalled helmets fail to meet essential safety standards for impact protection, labeling, and certification, which increases the risk of head injuries during accidents. Consumers are urged to stop using these helmets immediately and follow the outlined steps for a refund.
recalls.Title: Recall of NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, please contact TPBOR via email at nqdtpbor@outlook.com or visit their Amazon.com Seller Profile: TPBOR.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with federal safety regulations increases the risk of head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the helmets and contact TPBOR for a full refund. To process the refund, destroy the helmet by cutting the straps, take a photo of the destroyed helmet, and email it to nqdtpbor@outlook.com. After submitting proof of destruction, dispose of the helmet. TPBOR and Amazon will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.Description: Todson is recalling the Nerf Barrage Youth bike helmets due to concerns that they do not meet federal safety standards for retention systems. These helmets, featuring a blue and orange design, black straps, and an orange buckle, may fail to protect users in the event of a crash, increasing the risk of head injuries. The model number 6011 can be found on an internal label along with specific lot and reference numbers for the affected helmets. The recalled lots include LOT# BC01BJ01-200623 with REF# 5382-BL and LOT# BC01BJ01-111521 with REF# 4970-BL.
recalls.Title: Todson Issues Recall for Nerf Barrage Youth Bike Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Todson at 800-278-2565 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. They can also email NerfHelmetRecall@Todson.com or visit www.Todson.com/NerfHelmetRecall for more details.

The brand name "KORIMEFA" is displayed prominently on the front.

recalls.Description:
Bell Sports is recalling its Bell Soquel Youth helmets manufactured before September 2021 due to a potential safety hazard. These helmets, available in various colors across the U.S. and Canada, may not provide adequate protection in the event of a crash. The recalled models can be identified by the inner sticker featuring the name 'Soquel' and the code 'B0726Y', along with a date code of August 2021 ('8/21') or earlier. Several specific models are affected, including variations in color and design.

Consumers are urged to stop using these helmets immediately and reach out to Bell Sports for a refund. To facilitate the return process, consumers must destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, submit a photo of the destroyed helmet to verify destruction, and then dispose of it safely. The helmets were sold at major retailers and online platforms from January 2017 until January 2024, priced between $25 and $50.
recalls.Title: Bell Sports Recalls Bell Soquel Youth Bicycle Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bell Helmets at 800-456-2355 (Option 3) from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, or visit www.thebellgarage.com/bell-sports-inc-is-voluntarily-recalling-the-bell-youth-soquel-helmet for more details.

Products.Name: Bell Soquel Youth Helmet
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,425 (In addition, about 2,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bell Soquel Youth Helmet
Recalled Bell Soquel Youth Helmet Label

Retailers.Name: Available at Fred Meyer, Walmart, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.Amazon.com.

Importers.Name: Bell Sports Inc, of Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of head injury due to potential dislodging of the strap anchor, violating CPSC safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets and contact Bell Sports for a refund after destroying the helmet.

recalls.metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls Bell Soquel Youth helmets due to head injury risks. Immediate action required for refund and safety.

recalls.Description: Retrospec is recalling its Scout model kid's bike helmets available in sizes XS and S due to a potential risk of head injury. These helmets were offered in various colors and designs including Blippi, Blush, Brash Blue, Chameleon, Cool Mint, Matte Forest Green, Matte Black, Matte Navy, Matte White, Matte Burnt Orange, Matte Green, Matte Royal Blue, and Matte Whisper Pink. Each helmet features a green hang tag with the Retrospec name, Scout branding, and the helmet size, along with the Retrospec logo on the right side.
recalls.Title: Retrospec Recalls Scout Kid's Bike Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Retrospec at 888-488-2369 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://retrospec.com/pages/retrospec-scout-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Black)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Blue)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Green)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Pink)
Hang tag label on recalled helmet, listing the name “Retrospec,” the Scout brand name, and the helmet size

Retailers.Name: Available at T Scheels, Play it Again Sports, independent bicycle stores nationwide, and online at Retrospec.com and Amazon.com from February 2019 through March 2023, priced between $18 and $23.

Importers.Name: Xander Bicycle Corporation, DBA Retrospec, of Perris, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of head injury due to potential helmet failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets and contact Retrospec for a full refund. Destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, marking it with initials and the date, and upload a photo to the recall registration page to confirm destruction before disposal.

recalls.metaDescription: Retrospec recalls Scout kid's bike helmets due to head injury risks. Learn how to get a refund and safely dispose of the helmets.

recalls.Description: Bell Sports has issued a recall for the Slope adult bicycle helmets, which were available in dark green and light blue colors. These helmets can be identified by an inner sticker that reads 'Slope' along with the code B1161X. It is important to note that only adult-sized helmets are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Bell Sports Recalls Slope Adult Bicycle Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bell Helmets at 800-456-2355 (Option 3) from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com or visit www.thebellgarage.com and click on the recall banner for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bell Slope helmet (Dark Green)
Recalled Bell Slope helmet (Light Blue)

Retailers.Name: Available at Target, Fred Meyer, Academy, and other retailers nationwide, as well as various websites from January 2023 to April 2023 for approximately $30.

Hazards.Name: Potential for strap anchor dislodgment, violating CPSC safety regulations and increasing head injury risk in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the recalled helmets and contact Bell Sports for a refund. Consumers must destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, provide photographic proof to consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, and then dispose of the helmet.

recalls.metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls Slope adult bicycle helmets due to head injury risks. Stop using them and contact for a refund.
